title: INIT attribute not set on FDRE when shift register inference

features.name: wide register with explicit initializer in LFSR shift pattern
features.description: Code should include a multi-bit register (width > 16) explicitly initialized to a known value (e.g. zero), used in a shift register feedback loop where the shift-and-feedback logic is applied every clock edge

features.name: concatenation-based shift with multi-tap XOR/XNOR feedback
features.description: Code should include a non-blocking assignment using concatenation to perform a shift operation, where the inserted bit is computed from multiple taps of the same register combined with XNOR operators
features.snippet: state <= { state[23:1], state[24] ~^ state[23] ~^ state[22] ~^ state[17] };

features.name: register init value propagation across sequential optimization boundary
features.description: Code should include a register with a known binary initializer where the register feeds a shift-chain, so that synthesis tools must propagate the init attribute through shift-register inference optimization passes
